programmes.show_description: Stalwart of the drum and bass scene, Breakage, takes over the Sweatbox with 30 minutes of his own material.
Celebrating the eclectic remix catalogue of Kieran Hebden, better known as Four Tet.
Sit back and enjoy Divorce's very own curated Morning After Mix featuring Labi Siffre, Joni Mitchell and Mitski.
A hand-crafted, two-hour mix of chilled music old and new. With tracks from Frank Ocean, Scott Walker, Mitski, Little Dragon, The National and more.
Let Afrodeutsche be your guide into a sublime state of relaxation. Her musical flow is exactly what you need to start your Sunday morning just right.
Mark and Stuart present a feast of great music and chat for weekend breakfast, plus they celebrate the 50th anniversary of pioneering punk band the Ramones' debut album.
Cerys is joined by Tauseef Akhtar in session and film scholar Rachel Dwyer, exploring Bollywood's poetic traditions with live ghazals, rich cinema insight and archive moments.
Marc Riley is in for Guy, bringing you his Finest Flour (and no, that's not a misprint). He's even got a game you can play at home.
Jarvis takes you on a sonic trip through time and space, the Peak District and a village that may or may not exist.
Robert del Naja on Adam Curtis, plus world exclusive preview of new music from Loriane James.
Stuart's featured album is the self titled debut by German cult legends Faust. Plus, experimental jazz from Belgium and classic UK Psyche. Let's go!
Zakia pulls out some old favourites inspired by the recent burst of sunshine and is joined by south London producer Kwes to share the inspiration behind his second album, Kinds.
4/10. Live from the 6 Music Festival.
Live from the 6 Music Festival.
9/10. Live from the 6 Music Festival.
Soundtrack the early hours with back-to-back music, plus exclusive BBC sessions.
Emily Pilbeam presents a mixtape of her personal selection of tracks from BBC Introducing, with a new Track of the Week by Orla Noble.
It's Emily Pilbeam with a stack of great tunes for your Monday morning, plus there's a brand new 5 Before 6 DJ.
Actor Brian Cox joins Nick to talk about his directorial debut film, Glenrothan, in which he also stars.
Lauren gives you the finest blend of fantastic music for your weekday morning.
You've got absolutely nothing to worry about, because Chris is here to keep the tunes coming. Plus, he enters the Cave of Rave, before giving the Request Roulette Wheel a spin.
Steve Lamacq chats with, and has brand new music from, The Anchoress and also has the first play of new music from JJerome87 - aka Joe Newman from ALt-J.
Deb unveils this week's Breaking Artist, queer/trans electropop-bass duo deBasement, following the release of their single Aftermarket Bass featuring Nikki Nair.
Marc Riley and Gideon Coe come together to play their music loves from every era and genre.
1/4. Stuart Braithwaite of Mogwai shares the tracks that accompany him on his travels, spanning everything from his university days to the tunes he turns to while touring with his bandmates.
